description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular, boxy form and a woodgrain exterior finish. The front features a convex glass screen displaying a classic test pattern, surrounded by a dark, thick bezel. To the right of the screen are three prominent rotary control knobs arranged vertically, along with a small speaker grille above them. The back panel is plain woodgrain with a recessed area containing input/output ports and a small label. The sides and top are unadorned, showcasing the wood texture, while the overall design evokes mid-20th-century aesthetics.
description: A vintage, woodgrain television with a convex glass screen, rotary knobs, and a thick bezel, evoking a classic mid-century style.
description_view_0: The back view shows a plain woodgrain surface with a recessed panel for ports and a small label in the lower right corner.
description_view_1: The left side is a featureless woodgrain panel, highlighting the boxy shape and smooth finish.
description_view_2: The front view displays the convex glass screen with a test pattern, three rotary knobs, a speaker grille, and a thick, dark bezel.
description_view_3: The right side is another plain woodgrain panel, similar to the left, with no visible features.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, dark brown/black bezel with a woodgrain finish, rectangular and prominent
attribute_control_button_layout: three rotary knobs and one button arranged vertically on the front right side
